name: Reconcile WI-556 baseline gate flip with harness-hygiene CI restructure + give eval-live.yml a real call budget
description: Two CI changes to the same files are landing from different branches and must be reconciled. (1) WI-556 (branch WI-556, from main) seeds baseline.json and removes continue-on-error from the api-quality-gate.yml validate-baseline step. The unmerged harness-hygiene branch (WI-452 CI restructure, ~19 commits ahead of main as of 2026-06-09 23:16) rewrites api-quality-gate.yml (dorny filter -> change-class router) with the step comment 'validate-baseline stays continue-on-error pending WI-556 seed' — whichever merges second must NOT reintroduce continue-on-error:true. (2) harness-hygiene's new eval-live.yml runs 'pnpm eval:llm -- --live' and '--live --check-baseline' with NO --flow scope and NO --max-live-calls, so the default budget of 20 applies; the 4 envelope-emitting flows (exchanges/probes/safety-probes/language-quality, 232 matrix items, late in the FLOWS array) never execute, the run collects zero envelope metrics, and compareAgainstBaseline reports every seeded flow as a full-magnitude drop — the weekly drift check is structurally red even with a perfect baseline. Both eval-live steps need the envelope-flow scope and a sized budget (the seeded reference shape: --flow exchanges --flow probes --flow safety-probes --flow language-quality --max-live-calls 250), and timeout-minutes: 30 needs raising (232 sequential live calls measured ~35-40 min on 2026-06-09).
acceptanceCriteria: 1. After both branches are on main, the api-quality-gate.yml validate-baseline step has NO continue-on-error (blocking), regardless of merge order. 2. eval-live.yml's Tier-2 and drift-check steps run the 4 envelope-emitting flows with --max-live-calls 250 (or an equivalent explicit budget covering the 232-item matrix), and the job timeout accommodates the measured runtime. 3. A workflow_dispatch run of eval-live.yml after reconciliation completes the drift check against the committed baseline (green, or red only on genuinely new drift / the failures tracked by WI-558 and WI-559) — this also discharges WI-556 AC6.
riskImpact: P1 because the merge-order race can silently reintroduce a non-blocking baseline gate, and eval-live can remain structurally red without exercising the envelope flows, hiding LLM regressions or blocking release verification until manually reconciled.
